mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit balances signals broad-based demand expansion. This dual confirmation compresses credit spreads and lifts cyclical equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 15 basis points per month over a 3-year rolling window, or the cross-sectional correlation between HOUST and RSAFS drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above 102, supply chain bottlenecks clear and corporate margins expand. This operational leverage drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 102 for three consecutive months, indicating supply overshoot rather than margin expansion.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises faster than 2.5 percent year-over-year, the Federal Reserve signals imminent policy tightening. Anticipated rate hikes compress equity multiples and generate negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional IC remains above 0.05 despite the negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real gross domestic product reduces recession probabilities and supports corporate revenue growth. This fundamental improvement mechanically lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (p > 0.10), or the signal underperforms cash by more than 200 basis points annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms sustainable labor demand. This employment quality signal reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when PAYEMS adds fewer than 150,000 jobs monthly, proving the interaction relies on high absolute employment growth rather than rate stability.

mechanism_narrative: During quantitative easing periods, excess liquidity flows directly into housing markets. A sustained rise in housing permits signals wealth effects that boost consumer spending and drive positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST growth decouples from equity returns during liquidity injections, or forward returns turn negative when housing data misses consensus by more than 10 percent.
